sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.109 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: A non-trivial group word$w$is in a class$$if there is a function$f:$such that the width of$w$in every$d$-generator$$-group$G$is bounded by$f(d)$(i.\,e. every element of the verbal subgroup$w(G)$is equal to a product of$f(d)$values of$w$or their inverses). If$$is a class of finite groups, this is equivalent to saying that in every finitely generated pro-$$group$G$the verbal subgroup$w(G)$is closed....

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
